ARTICLE II .
There is hereby created The Breaks Interstate Park Com-
mission , which shall be a body corporate with the powers and
duties set forth herein and such additional powers as may be
conferred upon it by subsequent action of the appropriate au-
thorities of Kentucky and Virginia . The commission shall con-
sist of three commissioners from each of the two states , each of
whom shall be a citizen of the state he shall represent. Members
of the commission shall be appointed by the Governor . Va-
cancies shall be filled by the Governor for the unexpired term .
The term of one of the first commissioners appointed shall be for
two years, the term of another for three years , and the term of
the third for four years . Their successors shall be appointed
for terms of four years each. Each commissioner shall hold office
until his successor is appointed and qualified . An officer or
employe of the state , a political subdivision or the United States
government may be appointed a commissioner under this Act.
ARTICLE III .

strumentality of both the Commonwealth of Kentucky and the

scientifically sound safety features and their incorporation into
vehicles .
(b) The purposes of this compact are to :
( 1 ) Promote uniformity in regulation of and standards for
equipment .
( 2) Secure uniformity of law and administrative practice
in vehicular regulations and related safety standards to permit
incorporation of desirable equipment changes in vehicles in the
interest of greater traffic safety .
(3 ) To provide means for the encouragement and utiliza-
tion of research which will facilitate the achievement of the fore-
going purposes , with due regard for the findings set forth in
subdivision ( a) of this Article .
( c) It is the intent of this compact to emphasize perform-
ance requirements and not to determine the specific detail of
engineering in the manufacture of vehicles or equipment except
to the extent necessary for the meeting of such performance re-
quirements .
ARTICLE II
Definitions
As used in this compact :
( a) "Vehicle " means every device in, upon or by which any
person or property is or may be transported or drawn upon a
highway , excepting devices moved by human power or used ex-
clusively upon stationary rails or tracks .
(b ) "State” means a state , territory or possession of the
United States , the District of Columbia , or the Commonwealth
of Puerto Rico .
( c) "Equipment " means any part of a vehicle or any acces-
sory for use thereon which affects the safety of operation of such
vehicle or the safety of the occupants .

CHAPTER 101 357
ARTICLEX
Construction and Severability
This compact shall be liberally construed so as to effectuate
the purposes thereof . The provisions of this compact shall be
severable and if any phrase, clause, sentence or provision of this
compact is declared to be contrary to the Constitution of any
state or of the United States or the applicability thereof to any
government , agency, person or circumstance is held invalid , the
validity of the remainder of this compact and the applicability
thereof to any government , agency, person or circumstance shall
not be affected thereby . If this compact shall be held contrary
to the constitution of any state participating herein , the compact
shall remain in full force and effect as to the remaining party
states and in full force and effect as to the state affected as to all
severable matters .
Section 2. The legislature finds that :
1. The public safety necessitates the continuous develop-
ment, modernization and implementation of standards and re-
quirements of law relating to vehicle equipment , in accordance
with expert knowledge and opinion .
2. The public safety further requires that such standards
and requirements be uniform from jurisdiction to jurisdiction , ex-
cept to the extent that specific and compelling evidence supports
variation .
3. The Department of Public Safety, acting upon recom-
mendations of the Vehicle Equipment Safety Commission and
pursuant to the Vehicle Equipment Safety Compact provides a
just, equitable and orderly means of promoting the public safety
in the manner and within the scope contemplated by this act.

360 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
A. Whether the Constitution Revision Assembly will recommend guide-
lines to a constitutional convention to be called pursuant to Sections
258, 259 , 260, 261 , 262 , and 263 of the Constitution now in effect ,
or
B. Whether the Constitution Revision Assembly will recommend that
its proposed changes be made to the present constitution as an
amendment to the whole , or any part of parts thereof ; and
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it resolved by the Senate of the Commonwealth of Kentucky,
the House of Representatives concurring therein :
First : It shall be the public policy of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky to encourage the Constitution Revision Assembly
to deliberate upon constitutional reform without hindrance and to
afford the assembly the unimpeded opportunity to recommend
whatever method it deems best to the end that the results of the
assembly will be submitted to the electorate for ratification or
rejection .
Second : In recognition of the possibility that the Constitu-
tion Revision Assembly may recommend that the 1964 session of
the General Assembly in called session propose revision of the
present constitution in the form of an amendment to the whole , or
to any part or parts thereof , it is declared to be the policy of the
1964 regular session of the General Assembly that no amendment
to the present constitution be proposed for submission at this
session of the General Assembly which adjourns sine die on
March 20 , 1964 .
Signed March 19, 1964

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth

Section 1. KRS 395.515 is amended to read :
In such an action the petition must state the amount of the
debts and the nature and value of the property , real and personal ,
of the decedent , so far as known to the plaintiff ; if it appears that
there is a genuine issue concerning the right of any creditor ,
beneficiary or heir - at - law to receive payment or distribution , or
if it appears that there is a genuine issue as to what constitutes
a correct and lawful settlement of the estate , or a correct and
lawful distribution of the assets , such issues may be adjudicated
by the Court ; and , if it shall appear that the personal estate is
insufficient for the payment of all debts , the court may order the
real property descended or devised to the heirs or devisees who
may be parties to the action , or so much thereof as shall be
necessary , to be sold for the payment of the residue of such debts .

CHAPTER 106
(S. B. 35 )
AN ACT relating to appointment of Assistant Commonwealth's At-
torneys in judicial districts composed of only one county .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. In any judicial district composed of only one
county , containing a city of the second class , and having a popula-
tion of less than 200,000 , the Commonwealth's Attorney may ap-
point an assistant Commonwealth's Attorney for a term of four
years subject to removal at any time by the Commonwealth's
Attorney . He shall receive an annual salary of not less than
$4,000 , nor more than $6,000 , to be fixed by the fiscal court and
paid out of the county levy in equal monthly installments .

classification.title: Amendment to Section 108.040 of Kentucky Revised Statutes
classification.summary: This statute amends Section 108.040 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes, detailing the appointment, removal, and duties of a district director. It outlines the council's role in appointing and removing the director, requiring notice and a public hearing for removal. The director, as chief executive officer, is responsible for appointing and removing department heads with council approval, appointing and removing other employees, supervising their activities, and fixing their compensation, subject to council regulations. The director's compensation is also fixed by the council. Notably, no residence qualification is required for the director. The statute also includes provisions for the composition, election, and terms of the council, as well as its powers and duties, including establishing departments, meeting regularly, and adopting rules and regulations for the district's functioning. Members of the council are paid a salary fixed by the council.

Section 3. Section 108.050 of Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The question of the establishment of an urban services
district shall be submitted to the voters of the district if a petition
bearing the signatures of the number of registered voters of the
area to be embraced within the district equal to at least ten per-
cent of the vote cast for county judge in that district at the last
election is filed with the county court . After the effective date of
this act, the county court shall not receive or consider a petition
for the creation of a district which would contain less than ten
square miles of area. The election on creation of the district shall
be held at the next regular election if the petition is filed at least
ninety days prior to the date for the regular election . The petition
shall state that establishment of the district is being sought
pursuant to the provisions of this chapter , shall state the official
name by which the district shall be known , and shall prescribe
the territory intended to be included in the district .
(2) If the county court finds the petition is in proper order
and the creation of the district will be in accordance with the
provisions of this chapter , a certified copy of the order of the
county court directing that the question be submitted to a vote
shall be filed with the county court clerk at least forty -five days
prior to the next regular election . The county court clerk shall
give notice of the petition by publishing it in two consecutive
editions of a newspaper of general circulation within the proposed
district . The county court clerk shall cause the question to be
submitted to a vote at the election . The question shall be in the
following form: "Are you in favor of establishing -----

CHAPTER 124 397
Section 1. The following actions shall be commenced within
five years after the cause of action accrued :
( 1) An action upon a contract not in writing , express or
implied .
(2) An action upon a liability created by statute, when
no other time is fixed by the statute creating the liability .
(3) An action for a penalty or forfeiture when no time
is fixed by the statute prescribing it .
(4) An action for trespass on real or personal property .
(5) An action for the profits of or damages for withholding
real or personal property .
( 6) An action for the taking , detaining or injuring personal
property , including an action for specific recovery .
(7) An action for an injury to the rights of the plaintiff,
not arising on contract and not otherwise enumerated .
(8 ) An action upon a bill of exchange , check , draft or order ,
or any endorsement thereof , or upon a promissory note, placed
upon the footing of a bill of exchange .
(9 ) An action to enforce the liability of a steamboat or
other vessel .
(10) An action upon a merchant's account for goods sold
and delivered , or any article charged in such store account .
( 11 ) An action upon an account concerning the trade of
merchandise , between merchant and merchant or their agents .
( 12) An action for relief or damages on the ground of
fraud or mistake .
(13) An action to enforce the liability of bail .
(14) An action for personal injuries suffered by any person
against the builder of a home or other improvements . This cause
of action shall be deemed to accrue at the time of original oc-
cupancy of the improvements which the builder caused to be
erected.
Signed March 21 , 1964

CHAPTER 125

AN ACT relating to county jail building funds .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
( 1 ) To provide for a county jail, related capital improve-
ments and other necessary facilities for the enforcement of
criminal law in all counties containing a city of the first class a
county jail building fund may be established by resolution of the
fiscal court . The term "capital improvement " as used in this
section shall include , but not be limited to ( a) the purchase ,
construction , reconstruction , renovation , or remodeling of a jail or
related county buildings , related public projects or facilities , and
(b) the initial equipment of any newly acquired or newly -built
building , project or facility wherein any essential governmental
function of the county or other unit of local government may be
located or carried on . All funds authorized to be raised for such
county jail building fund shall be paid to the county treasurer
and shall be deposited into a revolving fund which may be created
under this section and designated as the County Jail Building
County and from which payments
shall be disbursed in accordance with general law for the con-
struction or rental or both of a jail , capital improvements or
facilities as authorized by this section . Provided , however , that
no funds shall be collected or paid to the county treasurer under
this section until the fiscal court of a county containing a city
of the first class has established a county jail building fund .

(2 ) In counties in which a county jail building fund has
been established , a tax of two dollars , in addition to costs and
fees provided by law, shall be levied upon convictions in each
criminal case in every court within the county , no matter what
the penalty , including violations of city ordinances , whether any
fine is paid into the State Treasury or not. The funds raised by
the tax on convictions in circuit court , quarterly court and magis-
trates courts shall be paid to the State Treasury as required by
law, but such funds shall then be paid monthly by the state

CHAPTER 127
(S. B. 187)
AN ACT relating to coroners and the duty of persons finding or possess-
ing a dead body .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
KRS 72.020 is amended to read :
(1 ) Any person finding or having in his possession the
body of any person slain , drowned or otherwise suddenly killed ,
or whose death occurred from unnatural cause without the at-
tendance of a physician , shall immediately notify the coroner , and
shall not remove the body nor anything therefrom until directed to
do so by the coroner or other authorized person .
(2) The coroner shall take possession of any objects or
articles which , in his opinion , may be helpful in establishing the
cause of death , and he can make or cause to be made such tests
and examinations of said objects as may be necessary or useful
in determining the cause of death . In the event that a criminal
prosecution arises , all such objects and articles together with re-
ports of any examinations made upon them , shall be retained by
the coroner until their production in evidence is required by the
prosecuting authority , unless otherwise directed by written order
of the court in which such prosecution is pending .
(3) Upon final disposition of each criminal prosecution
under this act, the court shall by appropriate written order dispose
of
allobjects retained under the provisions
ofthis act
.
Signed March 21
, 1964

Section 1. Section 394.010 of the Kentucky Revised

(1) As used in this chapter , unless the context requires
otherwise , "will" means a last will or testament, codicil , appoint-

to make recommendations to or direct the trustee as to any or

classification.title: AN ACT relating to wills.
classification.summary: This act amends Section 394.010 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes concerning wills. It clarifies the definition of a 'will' and specifies that certain reservations by a grantor in a trust, such as retaining income, principal, the right to modify or revoke the trust, or the right to direct the trustee's actions, do not invalidate the trust or make it a testamentary disposition. This amendment applies to writings executed after its enactment and to previously executed writings if the grantor is still living.
classification.reasoning: The statute concerns the legal definition and validity of wills and trusts, specifically addressing the rights of a grantor to retain control or benefit from a trust. 406 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
A new section to Chapter 190 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is hereby enacted to provide as follows :
No license shall be issued by the Department to any person
to act as a motor vehicle dealer , or as a motor vehicle salesman
for a motor vehicle dealer , unless the applicant for the motor
vehicle dealer license has a fixed and permanent place of business
in this State , including a building of suitable character and
adequate facilities for the purpose of performing the functional
business and duties of a motor vehicle dealer , which shall include
the books , records , files and equipment necessary to properly
conduct such business. The place of business shall not consist of
a residence , tent, temporary stand or open lot .
Signed March 21 , 1964
CHAPTER 130
(S. B. 212 )
AN ACT relating to the Uniform Commercial Code .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. Subsection ( 27 ) of KRS 355.1-201 is amended
to read :
(27) Notice, knowledge or a notice or notification received
by an organization is effective for a particular transaction from
the time when it is brought to the attention of the individual
conducting that transaction , and in any event from the time
when it would have been brought to his attention if the organiza-
tion had exercised due diligence . An organization exercises due
diligence if it maintains reasonable routines for communicating
significant information to the person conducting the transaction
and there is reasonable compliance with the routines . Due
diligence does not require an individual acting for the organiza-
tion to communicate information unless such communication is
part of his regular duties or unless he has reason to know of the
transaction and that the transaction would be materially affected
by the information .

classification.title: Amendments to KRS 355.3-104 and KRS 355.3-122
classification.summary: This statute amends two sections of the Kentucky Revised Statutes (KRS) related to negotiable instruments. Section 5. KRS 355.3-412 is amended to read:

(1) Where the drawee's proffered acceptance in any manner varies the draft as presented the holder may refuse the acceptance and treat the draft as dishonored in which case the drawee is entitled to have his acceptance cancelled.

(2) The terms of the draft are not varied by an acceptance to pay at any particular bank or place in the [continental] United States, unless the acceptance states that the draft is to be paid only at such bank or place.

(3) Where the holder assents to an acceptance varying the terms of the draft each drawer and indorser who does not affirmatively assent is discharged.

Section 6. KRS 355.3-504 is amended to read:

(1) Presentment is a demand for acceptance or payment made upon the maker, acceptor, drawee or other payor by or on behalf of the holder.

(2) Presentment may be made
    (a) by mail, in which event the time of presentment is determined by the time of receipt of the mail; or
    (b) through a clearing house; or
    (c) at the place of acceptance or payment specified in the instrument or if there be none at the place of business or residence of the party to accept or pay. If neither the party to accept or pay nor anyone authorized to act for him is present or accessible at such place presentment is excused.

(3) It may be made
    (a) to any one of two or more makers, acceptors, drawees or other payors; or
    (b) to any person who has authority to make or refuse the acceptance or payment.

(4) A draft accepted or a note made payable at a bank in the [continental] United States must be presented at such bank.

classification.summary: This statute amends KRS 355.6-103 to specify certain transfers that are not subject to the article, including those made for security, general assignments for creditors, settlement of security interests, sales by fiduciaries or public officers, corporate dissolution/reorganization sales, transfers to persons assuming debts with public notice, transfers to new business enterprises assuming debts, and transfers of exempt property. It also details the method for public notice.
classification.reasoning: The provided statute text discusses commercial transactions, specifically exceptions to an article related to transfers. It details various types of transfers that are not subject to the article, such as those for security, assignments for creditors, and sales by fiduciaries. There is no mention of race, nor any provisions that implicitly or explicitly discriminate based on race. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law. The document type is 'codes' as it refers to amending existing Kentucky Revised Statutes (KRS).

classification.title: Amendment to KRS 355.6-107 concerning bulk transfers
classification.summary: This statute amends KRS 355.6-107, which outlines the requirements for notifying creditors in bulk transfer transactions. It specifies the information that must be included in the notice, such as the names and addresses of the transferor and transferee, other business names used by the transferor, and whether all debts will be paid in full. It also references KRS 355.6-108 regarding auction sales and details the conditions under which a bulk transfer is ineffective against creditors, including the requirement for the transferee to obtain and preserve a list of creditors and a schedule of property, and to permit inspection or file these documents with the county clerk.

Section 14. KRS 355.7-210 is amended to read:
( 1) Except as provided in subsection ( 2 ) , a warehouse-
man's lien may be enforced by public or private sale of the goods
in bloc or in parcels , at any time or place and on any terms which
are commercially reasonable , after notifying all persons known
to claim an interest in the goods . Such notification must include
a statement of the amount due , the nature of the proposed sale
and the time and place of any public sale. The fact that a better
price could have been obtained by a sale at a different time or
in a different method from that selected by the warehouseman
is not of itself sufficient to establish that the sale was not made
in a commercially reasonable manner . If the warehouseman
either sells the goods in the usual manner in any recognized
market therefor , or if he sells at the price current in such market
at the time of his sale , or if he has otherwise sold in conformity
with commercially reasonable practices among dealers in the
type of goods sold , he has sold in a commercially reasonable
manner . A sale of more goods than apparently necessary to be
offered to insure satisfaction of the obligation is not commercially
reasonable except in cases covered by the preceding sentence .

classification.title: Amendment to KRS 355.7-210 concerning warehouseman's lien enforcement
classification.summary: This statute amends KRS 355.7-210, detailing the procedures for enforcing a warehouseman's lien. It specifies requirements for public or private sales, including notification of interested parties, and outlines conditions under which a sale is considered commercially reasonable. A distinction is made for goods stored by merchants versus other goods, with the latter having more stringent notification requirements (in-person or registered/certified letter).
classification.reasoning: The provided statute text discusses the enforcement of warehouseman's liens and the commercial reasonableness of sales of goods. It outlines procedural requirements for notification and sale methods. There is no mention of race, nor are there any provisions that implicitly discriminate based on race. The statute appears to be a standard commercial law provision. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law.

entries.ocr_text:
CHAPTER 130 415
(c) The notification must include an itemized statement
of the claim , a description of the goods subject to the lien , a
demand for payment within a specified time not less than ten
days after receipt of the notification , and a conspicuous statement
that unless the claim is paid within that time the goods will be
advertised for sale and sold by auction at a specified time and
place .
(d) The sale must conform to the terms of the notification .
(e ) The sale must be held at the nearest suitable place to
that where the goods are held or stored .
(f) After the expiration of the time given in the notifica-
tion , an advertisement of the sale must be published once a week
for two weeks consecutively in a newspaper of general circulation
where the sale is to be held. The advertisement must include a
description of the goods , the name of the person on whose account
they are being held , and the time and place of the sale. The sale
must take place at least fifteen days after the first publication .
If there is no newspaper of general circulation where the sale is
to be held , the advertisement must be posted at least ten days
before the sale in not less than six conspicuous places in the
neighborhood of the proposed sale .
( 3) Before any sale pursuant to this section any person
claiming a right in the goods may pay the amount necessary to
satisfy the lien and the reasonable expenses incurred under this
section . In that event the goods must not be sold , but must be
retained by the warehouseman subject to the terms of the receipt
and this article .
(4) The warehouseman may buy at any public sale pur-
suant to this section .
(5) A purchaser in good faith of goods sold to enforce a
warehouseman's lien takes the goods free of any rights of persons
against whom the lien was valid , despite noncomplicance by the
warehouseman with the requirements of this section .
(6) The warehouseman may satisfy his lien from the pro-
ceeds of any sale pursuant to this section but must hold the
balance , if any, for delivery on demand to any person to whom he
would have been bound to deliver the goods .

Section 15. KRS 355.8-102 is amended to read:

(1) In this article unless the context otherwise requires
    (a) A "security" is an instrument which
        1. is issued in bearer or registered form; and
        2. is of a type commonly dealt in upon securities exchanges or markets or commonly recognized in any area in which it is issued or dealt in as a medium for investment; and
        3. is either one of a class or series or by its terms is divisible into a class or series of instruments; and
        4. evidences a share, participation or other interest in property or in an enterprise or evidences an obligation of the issuer.
    (b) A writing which is a security is governed by this article and not by Uniform Commercial Code-Commercial Paper even though it also meets the requirements of that article. This article does not apply to money.
    (c) A security is in "registered form" when it specifies a person entitled to the security or to the rights it evidences and when its transfer may be registered upon books maintained for that purpose by or on behalf of an issuer or the security so states.
    (d) A security is in "bearer form" when it runs to bearer according to its terms and not by reason of any indorsement.
    [(2) "Proper form" means regular on its face with regard to all formal matters.]
    (2) [(3)] A "subsequent purchaser" is a person who takes other than by original issue.

Section 18. KRS 355.8-306 is amended to read:
( 1 ) A person who presents a security for registration of
transfer or for payment or exchange warrants to the issuer that
he is entitled to the registration , payment or exchange. But a
purchaser for value without notice of adverse claims who receives
a new , reissued or re -registered security on registration of transfer
warrants only that he has no knowledge of any unauthorized
signature ( KRS 355.8-311 ) in a necessary indorsement .
( 2) A person by transferring a security to a purchaser for
value warrants only that
( a ) his transfer is effective and rightful ; and
(b ) the security is genuine and has not been materially
altered; and
( c) he knows no fact which might impair the validity of
the security .
(3 ) Where a security is delivered by an intermediary known
to be entrusted with delivery of the security on behalf of another
or with collection of a draft or other claim against such delivery ,
the intermediary by such delivery warrants only his own good
faith and authority even though he has purchased or made
advances against the claim to be collected against the delivery .

classification.title: AN ACT relating to advancements to county sheriffs for expenses.
classification.summary: This act amends Section 64.140 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes concerning liens on the real property of sheriffs and other individuals acting as sheriff. It also specifies the fee for the County Court Clerk for filing and recording quietus.

CHAPTER 133 427
( 1 ) [ In counties containing a population 75,000 or over ,
the ] Any sheriff may be entitled to receive an advancement to
defray necessary official expenses and to apply to the payment
of the salaries of himself and his deputies and assistants . The
Commissioner of Finance shall , on the first day of each calendar
month , determine the necessary amount that may be advanced
to the sheriff , which amount shall not exceed one -twelfth of the
total fees collected by the sheriff for the preceding year and may
be for a less amount, and in no event shall it exceed $30,000 per
month . When approved by the Commissioner of Finance , a
warrant shall be drawn on the State Treasury in favor of the
sheriff for such advancement . At the end of each calendar year
the sum of such advancements shall be deducted from the part
of the total of fees and commissions paid into the State Treasury
by the sheriff , pursuant to Section 106 of the Constitution , that
is available for use for the payment of the salaries of the sheriff ,
his deputies and assistants , and his necessary office expenses .
(2 ) If the office of sheriff becomes vacant , the sums ad-
vanced under this section shall be charged against that part of
the fees and the commissions of the office of the sheriff that have
been , or shall be , paid into the State Treasury during the calendar
year in which the advancements were made , and that is available
for payment of the salaries of the sheriff , his deputies and
assistants , and necessary expenses of his office .
(3) If the total of fees and commissions paid into the State
Treasury by the sheriff for any calendar year is insufficient to
match the amount of advancements made to him , he and his
sureties shall be liable to the state for any excess of advancements
over the total of fees and commissions paid into the State
Treasury .
Signed March 21 , 1964

classification.title: Amendment to KRS 183.867 concerning airport zoning
classification.summary: This statute amends Section 183.867 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes, granting the commission authority to require public airports to file maps of their surrounding areas used for approach or landing. The commission will designate areas for zoning purposes based on federal aviation standards and can exercise jurisdiction over these areas for safe aircraft maneuvering and airport use. Local zoning bodies can retain jurisdiction for all other matters. The commission is also empowered to adopt regulations for the zoning of these areas and must maintain public files of these maps and regulations.
classification.reasoning: The statute discusses airport zoning regulations and the powers of a commission related to aviation safety. There is no mention of race, nor are there any provisions that implicitly suggest racial segregation or discrimination. The focus is purely on land use and safety around airports. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law.

CHAPTER 134 445
Section 25. Section 183.990 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
(1) Any person violating any of the provisions of this
chapter with respect to operation of aircraft , or violating the
provisions of any rule , regulation or ordinance adopted under
KRS 183.133 (6), shall be fined not less than ten dollars nor more
than one hundred dollars or imprisoned not more than ninety
days or both .
(2) Every person coming within the provisions of KRS
183.530 through KRS 183.590 and every officer , agent or em-
ployee of the holder of any certificate issued under KRS 183.530
to 183.590 and every other person who violates or causes , aids or
abets any violation , of any order , rule or regulation lawfully issued
pursuant to the authority granted by KRS 183.530 to 183.590
shall , upon conviction , be punished by a fine of not less than
one hundred dollars nor more than five hundred dollars . The
prosecution may be had in the circuit court of any county in which
the offense or any part thereof shall have occurred .
( 3 ) Each violation of the statutes pertaining to the State
Airport Zoning Commission or of any order , rule or regulation
promulgated pursuant thereto shall be punishable by a fine of not
less than one hundred dollars nor more than five hundred dollars
or imprisonment for not more than thirty days or both , and each
day a violation continues to exist shall constitute a separate
offense .
Section 26. Notwithstanding any other evidence of legis-
lative intent , it is hereby declared to be the controlling legisla-
tive intent , that if any provision of this Act , or the application of
this Act to any persons or circumstances , is held invalid, the re-
mainder of this Act and the application of such provision to
persons or circumstances , other than those as to which it is held
invalid , shall not be affected thereby .
Section 27. Sections 183.022 , 183.139 and 183.761 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes are repealed .
Signed March 21 , 1964

CHAPTER 135
(S. B. 238 )
AN ACT relating to the issuance of checks for salaries and changes in
the payroll system .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 45.340 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
Except where otherwise provided by law, checks for salaries
due to public officers and employes shall be issued weekly , semi-
monthly , monthly or for such other period of time not exceeding
thirty days which may be designated by the Department of Fi-
nance with the approval of the Governor . Checks shall be
tendered to an officer or employe only after he has completed
the work for which he is being paid and not later than fifteen
days after the designated pay period . The Department of Finance
is authorized to effect such changes in the payroll system at any
time as are necessary to pay salaries after work has been per-
formed . The department is further authorized to make such
changes as are necessary and desirable in the posting of payroll
expenditures and any other accounting and reporting procedures .
In connection with such changes in accounting and reporting
procedures , KRS 45.231 shall not be construed to prevent the
issuance of salary checks for services rendered during a preceding
fiscal year .
Signed March 21 , 1964

iners appointed by him . The board shall be composed of five

(7) "Home " means a dwelling or dwellings for not more

classification.title: ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
classification.summary: This section of the Acts of the General Assembly from 1964 details the procedures for electing and filling vacancies for directors of an association. It outlines the terms of service, the process for increasing or decreasing the number of directors, and the requirements for bonding individuals who handle money. Section 11 specifically mandates that any person appointed or elected to a position involving the receipt, payment, management, or use of association money must be bonded within thirty days of their appointment or election, with the bond amount determined by the directors and approved by a majority of them. The association is responsible for paying the premium on these bonds.

Notwithstanding the limitations of this subsection, loans may be
assigned with recourse to any Federal Home Loan Bank of which
the association is a member .
( 12) Associations may service mortgages. The maximum
principal amount of mortgages thus serviced by an association
at any one time shall not exceed two -thirds of the amount of the

classification.summary: This statute outlines the process and powers of a court-appointed conservator for an association. Directors and officers remain in office, but the conservator can remove them with the Commissioner's approval.

490 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
( 10) While the association is in the charge of a conservator ,
members of such association shall continue to make payments
to the association in accordance with the terms and conditions
of their contracts , and the conservator , in his discretion , may
permit savings account members to withdraw their accounts from
the association pursuant to the provisions of this Act or under
such rules and regulations as the commissioner may prescribe .
The conservator shall have power to accept savings accounts and
additions to savings accounts , but any such amounts received
by the conservator may be segregated if the commissioner shall
so order in writing ; if so ordered , such amounts shall not be sub-
ject to offset and shall not be used to liquidate any indebtedness
of such association existing at the time the conservator was ap-
pointed for it or any subsequent indebtedness incurred for the
purposes of liquidating the indebtedness of any such association
existing at the time such conservator was appointed . All expenses
of the association during such conservatorship shall be paid by
the association .
Section 55. ( 1 ) If in the judgment of the commissioner the
public interest requires it, he may apply to a court of general
jurisdiction in the county in which the home office of any asso-
ciation is located for the appointment of a receiver . Such court
is authorized to appoint a receiver if it finds that such association :
( a) Is in an impaired condition ; or
(b) Is in violation of an order or injunction , as provided in
Section 54 of this Act , which has become final in that the time
to appeal has expired without appeal or a final order entered from
which there can be no appeal . The commissioner , an examiner
or other person may be appointed by the court as receiver , and
a certified copy of the order of the court making such appointment
shall be evidence thereof . Such receiver shall have all the powers
and authority of a conservator , plus the power to liquidate , and
shall have such other powers and authority as may be expressed
in the order of the court . If the commissioner or an examiner is
appointed receiver , he shall receive no additional compensation ,
but if another person is appointed , then the compensation of the
receiver , as determined by the court , shall be paid from the assets
of the association .

classification.title: An Act relating to savings accounts and minors.
classification.summary: This act outlines the legal capacity of minors to enter into agreements with financial institutions regarding savings accounts. It specifies that minors can bind themselves to pledges or actions required by the institution, and that parents or guardians cannot transfer a minor's savings account unless the minor has died and the sum does not exceed $1,000, unless the minor has provided written notice to the institution. It also details the rules for joint savings accounts, allowing payment to any one of the account holders or survivors, and establishing the opening of such an account as conclusive evidence of intent to vest title in the survivor(s).

CHAPTER 140
(Н. В. 2)
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. Notwithstanding the provisions of KRS 141.010 ,
any taxpayer other than a trust may elect to deduct from taxable
income additional first year depreciation allowance as provided
in Section 179 of the Internal Revenue Code in effect on
January 1 , 1963. This provision shall apply to taxable years
beginning after December 31 , 1963 and to property purchased
after December 31 , 1957 .
Signed March 21 , 1964
CHAPTER 141
(Н. В. 3 )
AN ACT relating to the Kentucky Tax Commission .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
( 1 ) There is hereby created an administrative review
agency which shall be known as the "Kentucky Board of Tax
Appeals ."
( 2) All files , equipment , budget , and pending business of
the Kentucky Tax Commission are hereby transferred to the
Kentucky Board of Tax Appeals .
Section 2. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
(1 ) The Kentucky Board of Tax Appeals shall consist of
three members to be appointed by the Governor , with the consent
of the Senate . No member shall engage in any occupation or busi-
ness inconsistent with his duties as such a member .

Section 7. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
Appeals to the Kentucky Board of Tax Appeals shall be in
accordance with rules prescribed by the Board . All proceedings
before the Board shall be de novo . Findings by the Board shall
be restricted to those issues raised by the parties to the appeal .
In the event the Board finds that other issues are necessary to a
full determination of the controversy , it may remand the whole
proceeding to the agency from which the appeal was prosecuted
for further determination . The parties may stipulate to the deter-
mination of such other issues without remand .

classification.title: Amendment to KRS 138.340 concerning dealer and transporter licensing and reporting
classification.summary: This statute amends KRS 138.340, which deals with licensing and reporting requirements for dealers and transporters. It outlines penalties for filing false reports, failing to file reports, or failing to pay taxes. It also details procedures for license revocation, including notice, hearings, and appeals. Additionally, it specifies that revoked licenses are not transferable and that individuals whose licenses have been revoked are ineligible for new licenses for one year. It also includes provisions for manufacturers to keep records of cigarette shipments and for licensed individuals (except non-resident wholesalers) not to sell or purchase untaxpaid cigarettes from other licensees.

It allows the Department of Revenue to authorize or require separate accounting if the allocated net business income under the prescribed formula is substantially different from the actual net business income attributable to Kentucky, provided the accounting records clearly reflect this. It also amends Section 186.070 regarding the registration of motor vehicle manufacturers and dealers.
classification.reasoning: The provided text details amendments to Kentucky Revised Statutes concerning corporate income tax allocation and motor vehicle dealer registration. There is no mention of race, segregation, or any discriminatory practices. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law.

A motor vehicle bearing dealer's plates may be used only by a

or wholesale dealer in motor vehicles . Any dealer in motor
vehicles may allow any bona fide customer or the customer's
employes to use the dealer's tag on the motor vehicle that is being
demonstrated . This provision shall be limited to one trip or
demonstration to the same prospective customer .
(2) Each manufacturer and dealer when making applica-
tion for dealer's plates shall file a verified statement with the
clerk , giving the name and address of each member of the firm
and each bona fide salesman or employe entitled to the use of
the plates for demonstration purposes only. When any bona fide
salesman or employe so registered is no longer in the employ of
the firm, an amended verified statement shall be filed with the
clerk showing that fact, and when any additional salesmen or
employes are employed , an amended verified statement showing
their names and addresses shall be filed with the clerk so that the
records in the clerk's office will at all times show the bona fide
salesmen and employes actually in the service of the registered
dealer or manufacturer .
(3) The license of any dealer or manufacturer may be
revoked by the Department of Revenue for the violation of any
of the provisions of this section . Such manufacturer or dealer
shall be given an opportunity to be heard in defense of the charge
that he has violated any of the provisions of this section and the
Department of Revenue shall adopt and promulgate rules and
regulations governing the procedure for such revocation . Pro-
vided , however , that any manufacturer or dealer whose license
is so revoked may prosecute an appeal to the Kentucky Board of

classification.title: CHAPTER 141 525
classification.summary: This statute outlines the regulations for obtaining and using dealer's plates for motor vehicles. It details the fees for registration and additional plates, specifies who is permitted to use dealer plates (members of the firm, bona fide salesmen or employees, or for transporting vehicles), and requires manufacturers and dealers to file verified statements with the clerk listing eligible individuals. The statute also includes provisions for revoking a dealer's or manufacturer's license for violations and outlines the appeal process.
classification.reasoning: The provided statute text discusses regulations for motor vehicle dealer plates, including registration fees, authorized users, and license revocation procedures. There is no mention of race, nor are there any provisions that implicitly suggest racial discrimination. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law. The document type is 'session_laws' as indicated by the 'Gen Assemb 1964.pdf' source and the chapter numbering format.

classification.title: An Act relating to motor vehicle dealers and the Department of Revenue.
classification.summary: This act details the administrative procedures for the Department of Revenue concerning motor vehicle dealer licenses. It outlines penalties for violations, including license suspension and revocation, and specifies procedures for notification, hearings, and appeals. It also addresses the transfer of vehicle registrations for used motor vehicles acquired by dealers.

The penalties and procedures described apply universally to all motor vehicle dealers. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law. classification.summary: This statute amends KRS 132.140 concerning the taxation of spirits and KRS 133.120 regarding taxpayer appeals of assessments. It also includes a provision, enclosed in brackets, that was likely added or modified in the 1964 session, detailing procedures for revoking motor vehicle dealer licenses and canceling plates if a dealer fails to transfer registration of acquired used vehicles before resale. The bracketed text appears to be a separate, though related, amendment or addition to the existing statutes.

Section 33 amends KRS 132.140 concerning the taxation of spirits, and Section 34 amends KRS 133.120 regarding taxpayer appeals of assessments. The bracketed text between Section 33 and Section 34 appears to be a separate provision related to motor vehicle dealer licenses and the transfer of used vehicle registrations. None of these provisions, including the bracketed text, contain any explicit or implicit racial indicators or relate to the typical categories of Jim Crow laws such as segregation, voting rights, or marriage restrictions. classification.title: An Act relating to taxation and revenue.
classification.summary: This statute outlines the process for reviewing and changing property tax assessments. It details the roles of the board of supervisors, tax commissioner, Department of Revenue, county judge, city finance officer, and school district superintendent in the assessment and appeal process. It also grants the board of supervisors the power to issue subpoenas and administer oaths.

It outlines the powers and responsibilities of various government officials and boards involved in this process. There is no mention of race, nor are there any provisions that implicitly or explicitly discriminate based on race. Therefore, this statute is not a Jim Crow law.

classification.title: An Act relating to revenue and taxation and amending KRS 365.370.
classification.summary: This act amends Section 365.370 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes, granting the State Department of Revenue the authority to prescribe rules and regulations for the enforcement of KRS 365.260 to 365.380. It also empowers the department to conduct cost surveys and use them as provided in KRS 365.320(2) and 365.360(2). Additionally, the department is authorized to revoke or suspend licenses issued under these provisions after notice and hearing. The act also details penalties for violations of various KRS sections related to taxes and motor vehicles.

It outlines the powers of the State Department of Revenue regarding rule-making, cost surveys, and license enforcement. The penalties described are for general violations of tax and motor vehicle statutes and do not contain any explicit or implicit racial distinctions. Therefore, this statute is not a Jim Crow law.

Section 1. Section 117.615 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
Any person , male or female , may register as a voter during
the period the registration books are open if he possesses or will
possess on the day of the next regular election , the qualifications
set forth in Constitution 145. Any member of the armed services
while on extended duty may register not later than twenty days
[at any time ] before the election, provided he possesses the
necessary qualifications .
Section 2. Section 117.620 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
(1) Any person possessing the qualifications for registration
as a legal voter may appear before the county clerk of the county
of his residence or at the temporary branch offices authorized by
the county board of registration and purgation , or at the permanent
office or a branch office of the board of registration commissioners
if he resides in a city of the first class , and register, or change his
party affiliation , at any hour of any day that the office of the
county clerk or said temporary branch office or the board of
registration commissioners is open for business except as otherwise
provided in subsection ( 4) of this section . The county clerk of
every county and the board of registration commissioners of
every city of the first class shall cause all registration books or
lists to be closed fifty -nine days prior to, and five days following ,

classification.title: Section 10. Section 119.200 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended to read as follows
classification.summary: This statute amends Section 119.200 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes concerning qualifications for voting in primary elections. It requires voters to be registered members of the party whose ballot they wish to vote, and to have been registered as such at the time of the preceding regular election, or have registered and remained registered as a member of that party after the preceding regular election if no change in affiliation occurred. It also states that minors who will become eighteen years of age before the regular election are entitled to vote in the primary if otherwise qualified. The statute also outlines a procedure for determining a voter's qualifications in case of doubt by having an election judge swear the person in.

CHAPTER 143
(Н. В. 46)
AN ACT relating to Fish and Wildlife Resources .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. KRS 150.330 is amended to read:
(1) No person shall take any wild birds except during the
open season prescribed by the department for the particular
species .
[The open season for quail may be between November 1 ,
and February 1 only, and shall not exceed a total of sixty days ,
consecutive or otherwise . The open season for ruffed grouse or
native pheasant may be between November 1 and February 1
only, but shall not exceed a total of sixty days, consecutive or
otherwise .]
There shall be no open season for wild turkey , exotic pheasants or
chukar partridge until the commissioner , with the approval of the
commission , finds the conditions and population warrant it. Then
regulations shall be enacted to govern same . No person shall take
quail , ruffed grouse or native pheasant except from sunrise to
sunset .
(2) No person shall take , pursue , possess , transport , purchase
or sell or attempt to do so, any migratory birds , except as author-
ized by the Migratory Bird Treaty Act (40 Stat. 755) and regula-
tions under it.

classification.title: AN ACT relating to Fish and Wildlife Resources
classification.summary: This act amends KRS 150.330 to regulate the hunting seasons for various wild birds. It removes specific dates for quail and ruffed grouse seasons, stating they can only be hunted between sunrise and sunset. It also establishes that there will be no open season for wild turkey, exotic pheasants, or chukar partridge until the commissioner, with commission approval, deems conditions and populations warrant it, at which point regulations will be enacted. The act also reiterates restrictions on taking migratory birds as authorized by the Migratory Bird Treaty Act.

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. KRS 80.010 is amended to read :
As used in this chapter , unless the context otherwise requires :
( 1 ) "Housing project " means a building or buildings con-
taining rooms to be provided as living quarters , together with
shops , stores , garages , laundries , doctors' and dentists' offices and
other facilities and appurtenances deemed reasonably necessary
by the housing commission to the successful and economical
operation of the project . It also means any work or undertaking
of a housing commission or of the Federal Government to :
( a) Demolish , clear or remove a building or buildings from
any slum area , including the adaptation of such area to recrea-
tional , community or other public purposes ;
( b ) Provide decent , safe and sanitary living accommoda-
tions for persons who lack the amount of income that is necessary ,
as determined by the commission undertaking the project , to
enable them , without financial assistance , to obtain such accom-
modations ; such work or undertaking may include buildings , land
equipment , facilities and other real or personal property for
necessary , convenient or desirable appurtenances , streets , sewers ,
water service , parks , site preparation , gardening , administrative ,
community , health , recreational , welfare or other purposes ;
( c) Accomplish a combination of the foregoing .
( 2) "Housing commission " or " commission" means any
housing commission created pursuant to this chapter .
( 3) "Public body" means any city, village , county , com-

America , the United States Housing Authority and

shall, when all taxes, fees and charges have been paid as required by law, record one original in his office, and issue a certificate of incorporation. There shall be paid to the Secretary of State a fee of $3.00 for recording the articles, and a fee of $1.00 for issuing a certificate of incorporation.

(2) The certificate of incorporation together with the remaining original articles of incorporation, endorsed with the fact and time of recording in the office of the Secretary of State, shall be returned to the *incorporator or incorporators or his or their* representatives. One original shall then be filed with and recorded by the clerk of the county court of the county in which the registered office of the corporation is situated, and the other shall be retained by the corporation. The clerk of the county court shall be paid a fee of $3.00 for recording said articles.

(3) No foreign corporation, except a foreign insurance company, shall be authorized to do business in this state until it has filed a certified copy of its articles of incorporation and all existing amendments in the office of the Secretary of State. It shall thereafter file a certified copy of subsequent amendments to its articles in the office of the Secretary of State. Such corporations shall pay to the Secretary of State an original filing fee of $25.00 and a recording fee of $10.00 for recording the articles of incorporation and accompanying amendments, and shall pay the same recording fee for recording subsequent amendments.

(4) A copy of any articles of incorporation and all amendments thereto of both domestic and foreign corporations as recorded in the office of the Secretary of State, under the certificate and seal of that office, shall be accepted as evidence in any court for any purpose in any action in which the original could be used. For furnishing a certified copy of the record to any party, the Secretary of State shall charge the same fee to be charged for the recordation of that instrument.

classification.summary: This section of the Acts of the General Assembly of 1964 amends KRS 271.345. It outlines the general powers of a corporation, including the ability to sue and be sued, contract, acquire and dispose of property, appoint officers, make bylaws, conduct business, dissolve, acquire corporate shares, indemnify directors and officers, accept gifts, and make donations. classification.summary: This act creates new sections of Chapter 319 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes concerning the licensing and regulation of psychologists. It outlines procedures for license renewal, suspension, revocation, and refusal to issue or renew licenses based on various professional conduct issues, including felony convictions, fraud, deceit, professional misconduct, habitual drunkenness or drug addiction, negligence, acting outside the area of certification, or practicing without proper supervision. It also specifies notice requirements for disciplinary actions.

KRS 401.010 is amended to read as follows :
Any person at least twenty -one years of age, who is not
a married woman , may have his name changed by the county
court of the county in which he resides. If he resides on a
United States army post , military reservation or fort his name

thereto .
KRS 401.020 is amended to read as follows :
The father , or if he is dead or has abandoned his infant
child , the mother and guardian , or if no mother is living , the
guardian alone, may have the name of an infant child changed
by the county court of the county in which the child resides .
If the child resides on a United States army post, military reserva-
tion or fort his name may be changed by the county court of any
county adjacent thereto .
CHAPTER 156
( Н. В. 263 )

AN ACT relating to the probation of wills .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
KRS 394.140 is amended to read as follows :
Wills shall be proved before , and admitted to record by ,
the county court of the testator's residence (if such residence
was on a United States army post , military reservation or fort ,
it may be proved and admitted to record in the county court
of any county adjacent thereto); if he had no known place of
residence in this state , and land is devised , then in the county
where the land or part thereof lies ; if no land is devised , then
in the county where he died , or where his estate or part thereof
is , or where there is a debt or demand owing to him .
Signed March 21 , 1964

classification.title: AN ACT relating to the probation of wills.
classification.summary: This act amends Kentucky Revised Statutes (KRS) 401.010, 401.020, and 394.140. KRS 401.010 and 401.020 concern name changes for adults and infants, respectively, allowing for county court jurisdiction based on residence, including adjacent counties to U.S. Army posts. KRS 394.140 pertains to the probation and recordation of wills, specifying county court jurisdiction based on the testator's residence, with provisions for cases involving U.S. Army posts, out-of-state residences, or devised land.

CHAPTER 158
(Н. В. 271)
AN ACT relating to the protection of forests and to forest fire control .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. The General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky declares as a public policy of the Commonwealth the
prevention and control of forest fires on or threatening the forest
land within the Commonwealth in order to preserve forest and
other natural resources , enhance the growth and maintenance of
forests , conserve forest cover on watersheds , protect recreational ,
wildlife and other values , promote stability of forest -using indus-
tries , and prevent loss of life and damage to property from wild-
fires and other conflagrations .

(5) Deposit or leave unattended on any railroad or highway right-of-way any fire or live coals, or set or maintain thereon any fire for the purpose of cooking, heating, or providing light or warmth unless such fire is enclosed within a stove, drum, fireplace or other properly prepared location.

(6) Set a backfire or cause a backfire to be set, except under the direct supervision of the Division of Forestry, or unless it can be established that the setting of such backfire is necessary for the purpose of saving life or valuable property.

(7) Without authority, destroy, deface, or remove any notice, sign, or poster of the Division of Forestry, posted for the better protection of wood lots, forests or wild lands from fire or trespass.

(8) Use or operate on or adjacent to forest land, a welding torch, tarpot, or other device which may cause a fire, without clearing flammable material surrounding the operation or without taking such other reasonable precautions necessary to ensure against the starting and spreading of fire.

(9) Light, maintain or use a fire for cooking, heating or providing light or warmth upon lands not his own without first obtaining permission from the owner, lessee or agent thereof.

(10) Light or maintain on or adjacent to forest land, a bonfire within one hundred fifty feet (150 feet), of any house or building.

(11) Discharge or cause to be discharged a gun firing incendiary or tracer bullets or tracer charge or combustible gun wadding onto or across any forest land.

(12) Have in his possession on forest land any incendiary or tracer bullet or tracer charge, except in the course of transporting the same in conformity with law.

(13) Light or maintain on or adjacent to forest land an open fire or campfire without clearing flammable material surrounding such fire as necessary to ensure against the escape or spread thereof, or leave such fire before it is extinguished.

provisions of subsection ( 1 ) shall be effective only during the time

affected .

classification.title: AN ACT relating to quarterly courts.
classification.summary: This act amends KRS 25.480 to allow the judge of the quarterly court to appoint a clerk. The clerk's duties include filing papers, issuing processes, administering oaths, issuing warrants of arrest and criminal summons for offenses within the jurisdiction of the county and quarterly courts, taking appearance bonds, other bonds or recognizances, accepting payment of fines and bond forfeitures, and keeping court records. The clerk will be paid for their services in addition to other fees allowed by law. The act also repeals several sections of the Kentucky Revised Statutes related to fire prevention and penalties.
classification.reasoning: The provided statute text details amendments to laws concerning quarterly courts and the appointment and duties of a clerk. It also lists several repealed sections related to fire offenses. There is no mention of race, nor are there any provisions that implicitly suggest racial discrimination. Therefore, this statute is not a Jim Crow law.

classification.title: AN ACT relating to employes of sanitation districts.
classification.summary: This act authorizes the board of directors of any sanitation district to form their employees into groups for the purpose of obtaining group life insurance. Premiums can be paid by the employee, through payroll deduction, or a combination. The policy may also include accidental death and dismemberment insurance and can cover various classes of employees with different provisions.
classification.reasoning: The statute concerns the ability of sanitation districts to offer group life insurance to their employees. There is no mention of race, nor are there any provisions that would implicitly create racial distinctions or segregation. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law.

CHAPTER 163 593
Section 1. KRS 433.720 is amended to read:
(1) Any person who, without the consent of the owner ,
enters any orchard , game preserve , ginseng garden , [farm ] or
other premises enclosed by a fence not less than seven feet in
height , after the owner has conspicuously displayed on the
premises on a board not less than twelve by twenty -four inches
in size the word "posted"; and any person who damages any
such fence enclosing such premises shall be confined in the
penitentiary for not less than one nor more than three years .
(2) Any person who , without the consent of the owner
willfully enters or goes upon the enclosed farm premises of
another , whereon the owner , or other person in charge of the
premises has previously posted plainly written signs or warn-
ings thereon , where they may be readily seen , no more
than four hundred yards apart along or near the boundaries
of the premises, using the words " no trespassing," "keep out"
or "posted " or words of similar meaning or any person who
molests , injures , removes or destroys any such posted sign or
warning by the owner or person in charge of the premises
shall be punished by a fine of not more than five hundred dollars ,
or by confinement in the county jail for not more than six months ,
or both .
Signed March 21 , 1964

classification.summary: This statute outlines procedures for the temporary admission of individuals to state hospitals for the mentally ill or mentally retarded who require immediate treatment but lack the necessary paperwork. The statute also details the criteria for initial admission, requiring a physician or psychologist to certify the person's need for immediate care and their inability to apply for admission themselves.

AN ACT relating to definitions and the establishment of the Women's

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 197.010 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
Definitions as used in this chapter , unless the context other-
wise require :
( 1 ) "State Agency ” means any department , board , com-
mission or agency of the state government ;
( 2 ) "Department " means Department of Corrections ;
( 3 ) "Penitentiaries " includes the state penal institutions for
males at Eddyville and LaGrange , the institution for Women
located in Shelby County , together with the branches thereof
[in Shelby and Franklin Counties ] and any other similar institu-
tions hereafter established .
Section 2. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
( 1 ) The institution for women shall be known as " The
Kentucky Correctional Institutional for Women . "
Signed March 21 , 1964

classification.title: CHAPTER 167
classification.summary: This statute outlines regulations for livestock brands in Kentucky. It establishes procedures for brand registration, evidence of ownership, and the handling of animals with questionable ownership. It also reserves specific brands for the state and defines their meaning in relation to animal health (Brucellosis, Tuberculosis, vaccination). The statute further details the sale, assignment, and transfer of brands, requiring recorded transfers and written documentation upon sale of branded livestock.

classification.title: CHAPTER 168
classification.summary: This statute defines what constitutes 'wages' for the purposes of unemployment insurance or similar labor-related statutes. It explicitly excludes gratuities, payments made under employer plans for retirement, sickness, accident disability, medical expenses, or death benefits, payments made without deduction of federal taxes, and certain dismissal payments. It also excludes remuneration exceeding a certain threshold for the purposes of KRS 341.260 to 341.290.

Section 2. Section 341.250 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1) Any employing unit that becomes subject to this
chapter within any calendar year shall be considered a subject
employer during the whole of that calendar year , except as
specifically provided in subsections ( 3 ) and (4) of this section
or elsewhere in this chapter .
( 2 ) Except as provided in subsections ( 3 ) and ( 5 ) of this
section , a subject employer shall cease to be a subject employer
only as of the first day of January of any calendar year if he
files with the Division, on or before [prior to the fifteenth
[first] day of April [March ] of that year , a written application
for termination of coverage, and the covered employment per-
formed for such subject employer within the preceding calendar
year was not sufficient to render an employing unit a subject
employer under subsections ( 1 ) , ( 2 ) , ( 3 ) and ( 4 ) [ and ( 6 ) ]
of KRS 341.070 . The Commissioner may , however , after notify-
ing such employer in writing at his last known address , terminate
the coverage of any subject employer as of the first day of Janu-
ary of any calendar year if such subject employer has had no
individuals in covered employment in this state at any time
during the three preceding calendar years, and the balance of
such employer's reserve account may be immediately transferred
to the pooled account .

classification.title: CHAPTER 168 605
classification.summary: This statute outlines the conditions under which an employing unit can elect to become a subject employer for unemployment insurance purposes. It details the process for voluntary election, the duration of such elections, and the conditions under which an employing unit can cease to be a subject employer. It also addresses situations where services not normally considered covered employment can be elected as covered employment. Finally, it mentions that an employing unit becoming a subject employer under other subsections of KRS 341.070 becomes subject as of the date of acquisition, and that a subject employer whose reserve account is transferred to a successor immediately ceases to be a subject employer.
classification.reasoning: The statute discusses unemployment insurance regulations, specifically the election process for employers to become subject to the chapter and the conditions for ceasing to be a subject employer. There is no mention of race, and the provisions apply generally to all employing units. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law. The document type is 'codes' as it refers to 'Kentucky Revised Statutes' (KRS) and appears to be part of a compiled body of laws.

606 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
( 1 ) According to the circumstances in each case , a worker
shall be disqualified from receiving benefits or serving a waiting
period :
( a ) For not less than one nor more than sixteen weeks
immediately following the commission of the disqualifying act ,
if, subsequent to his most recent work , he has failed without good
cause either to apply for available , suitable work when so directed
by the employment office or the Commissioner or to accept suit-
able work when offered him , or to return to his customary self-
employment when so directed by the Commissioner ; or
(b) For not less than six nor more than sixteen weeks
immediately following the commission of the disqualifying act ,
if he has been discharged for misconduct connected with his
most recent work, but legitimate activity in connection with labor
organizations or failure to join a company union shall not be
construed as misconduct .
(2 ) A worker shall be disqualified from receiving benefits
or serving a waiting period for the duration of any period of un-
employment with respect to which :
( a ) He was discharged for dishonesty in connection with
his most recent work ;
(b ) He voluntarily quit his most recent work to marry or
for the purpose of attending school , or to become self- employed ;
or
( c ) He has left his most recent suitable work voluntarily
without good cause .
( 3 ) A worker shall be disqualified from receiving benefits
for any week with respect to which he knowingly made a false
statement to establish his right to or the amount of his benefits ;
and , within the succeeding twenty -four months , for such addi-
tional weeks immediately following the date of discovery [such
determination ] , not to exceed a total of fifty -two , as may be de-
termined by the Commissioner .
(4) No worker shall be disqualified under paragraph (b )
of subsection ( 1 ) or subsection ( 2 ) of this section unless the
employer , within a reasonable time as prescribed by commission
regulation , notifies the Division and the worker in writing of
the alleged voluntary quitting or the discharge for misconduct ;

classification.title: An Act relating to unemployment compensation
classification.summary: This statute outlines the conditions under which a worker can be disqualified from receiving unemployment benefits or serving a waiting period. Disqualifications can occur for failing to apply for or accept suitable work, being discharged for misconduct (with exceptions for labor union activity), dishonesty, voluntarily quitting to marry or attend school, or voluntarily leaving suitable work without good cause. It also details requirements for employer notification.
classification.reasoning: The provided statute text details regulations for unemployment benefits in Kentucky. It outlines reasons for disqualification such as misconduct, voluntary quitting, and dishonesty. There is no mention of race, nor are there any provisions that implicitly discriminate based on race. The statute appears to be a general labor law concerning unemployment compensation. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law.

classification.title: Kentucky Revised Statutes, Chapter 341 (Unemployment Compensation)
classification.summary: This section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes, specifically KRS 341.540 and related subsections from the 1964 Acts, details the charging of unemployment benefits against employer reserve accounts and the transfer of such accounts in cases of business reorganization. It outlines conditions under which benefits are charged to a pooled account rather than an individual employer's reserve account, such as voluntary quitting without good cause or discharge for misconduct. It also addresses the assumption of reserve accounts by successors in business.

CHAPTER 169
(Н. В. 403 )
AN ACT relating to the "friend of the court ."
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. KRS 403.090 is amended to read:
( 1 ) The fiscal court of any county may , by resolution , au-
thorize the appointment of a "friend of the court " . If the circuit
court of the county has but one judge , the appointment shall be
made by the judge . If the court has two or more judges , the
appointment shall be made by joint action of the judges , at the
general term . The appointment shall be for a term of two years ,
subject to removal at the pleasure of the appointing authority .
Vacancies shall be filled by appointment of the appoint-
ing authority for the unexpired term . The person appointed shall
be a licensed practicing attorney . He shall take the Constitutional
oath of office and shall give bond in such sum as may be fixed
by the appointing judge or judges .
( 2) It shall be the duty of the friend of the court to super-
vise and enforce the payment of sums ordered or adjudged by
the circuit court in divorce actions to be paid for the care and
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1964, p. 631

classification.title: CHAPTER 169
classification.summary: This statute outlines the role and responsibilities of a "friend of the court" in divorce actions involving minor children. It details how payments for child support and maintenance should be handled, including potential disbursement through the juvenile court. The friend of the court is tasked with investigating cases of delinquent payments, ensuring proper care and maintenance of children, and reporting any deficiencies to the court. In divorce cases with minor children, the friend of the court may be requested to investigate and report on matters affecting the children's rights and interests to aid the court in making just orders regarding their care, custody, and maintenance.
classification.reasoning: The statute concerns the administration of child support and custody arrangements in divorce proceedings. It establishes the role of a 'friend of the court' to ensure payments are made and children are properly cared for. There is no mention of race, nor are there any provisions that implicitly or explicitly discriminate based on race. The statute appears to be a procedural or civil law related to family matters and does not align with the characteristics of Jim Crow laws, which were designed to enforce racial segregation and discrimination.
